**Yellow cross** (now with player at center):
- Node 9: Y, 11px, vertical upper arm [16,21]-[26,21]
- Node 16: Y, 11px, horizontal left arm [27,10]-[27,20]

- Node 20: b, 53px - the entire blue cross as one merged shape
So SPACE teleported the player from the blue cross center to the yellow cross center. The blue cross is still there but now as a single merged node (53 pixels = 4 arms × 13 + 1 center = 53).
The yellow cross arms are each 11 pixels (from center to tip, not including center). So the yellow cross extends:
- Up: rows 16-26 at col 21

- Horizontal: row 27, cols 10-32

- [9, 24]: row 9, col 24 - NOT on cross
- [17, 15]: row 17, col 15 - NOT on cross (row 17 is within 16-38 but col 15 is not 21)
None of the yellow targets are on the yellow cross currently. So I need to move the yellow cross to overlap them.
Wait, but the cross moves 3 cells per step. Let me check which targets are reachable:
From [27, 21], moving in 3-cell increments:

- Cols: 21, 18, 15, 12, 9, 6, 3, 0 (left) and 24, 27, 30, 33, 36, 39, 42, 45, 48, 51, 54, 57, 60, 63 (right)
Yellow targets:
- [3, 15]: row 3 ✓, col 15 ✓ - REACHABLE!
- [9, 6]: row 9 ✓, col 6 ✓ - REACHABLE!
- [9, 24]: row 9 ✓, col 24 ✓ - REACHABLE!
- [17, 15]: row 17 - NOT on 3-cell grid (15, 18 are)
Hmm, [17, 15] has row 17 which is not on the 3-cell grid. But the cross arms extend 11 cells from center, so maybe the cross can overlap targets even if the center isn't exactly on them.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The cross arms are 11 pixels long. So if the center is at [27, 21]:
- Vertical arm covers rows 16-38 at col 21
- Horizontal arm covers cols 10-32 at row 27
For a target to be "collected", it needs to be on one of the cross's arms. So:
- [3, 15]: Would need center at row 3+11=14 or 3-11=-8 (up arm) or center at col 15+11=26 or 15-11=4 (left/right arm)
  - Actually, the target needs to be ON the cross. So for the vertical arm: target col must equal center col, and target row must be within [center_row-11, center_row+11]. For the horizontal arm: target row must equal center row, and target col must be within [center_col-11, center_col+11].
Let me reconsider. The cross has a vertical arm and a horizontal arm. A target is collected if it falls on either arm.
For the yellow cross centered at [27, 21]:
- Vertical arm: col 21, rows 16-38
- Horizontal arm: row 27, cols 10-32
Yellow target [17, 15]:

- On horizontal arm? row 17 ≠ 27, NO

To collect [17, 15] with the vertical arm: center col must be 15, and 17 must be within [center_row-11, center_row+11]
To collect [17, 15] with the horizontal arm: center row must be 17, and 15 must be within [center_col-11, center_col+11]

- Possible cols: ..., 12, 15, 18, 21, 24, ...

- Using horizontal arm: center at [17, col] where 15 is in [col-11, col+11]. But 17 is not on the 3-cell grid.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: SPACE teleports player between blue cross and yellow cross centers. Player is now at yellow cross center [27,21]. Yellow cross arms extend 11 cells each direction. Cross moves 3 cells per arrow press. Goal: collect matching-color targets by overlapping cross arms with them.
Plan: Move yellow cross LEFT twice to position center at [27,15], which should overlap the yellow target at [17,15] with the vertical arm.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move yellow cross LEFT twice to center at [27, 15]
# This should put the vertical arm at col 15, rows 16-38
# Target [17, 15] should be on the vertical arm

predict(expect_change=True, note='Moving LEFT twice should move yellow cross center from [27,21] to [27,15], overlapping yellow target at [17,15]')
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT'])

print(f"Step: {current_frame.step}")
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")

# Check player position and yellow cross
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"Player at: {n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 1:
        print(f"Yellow arm {n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if yellow targets changed
print("\n=== YELLOW TARGETS ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"Yellow target: {n['boundary']}")

# Check gray squares with yellow centers
print("\n=== GRAY SQUARES WITH YELLOW CENTERS ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['children']:
        child = seg['nodes'][n['children'][0]]
        if child['color'] == 'Y':
            print(f"Gray square at {n['boundary']} with yellow child at {child['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
